gloss_after: noun: mark made by scraping | verb: scrape a surface \| rub itching skin

def_before: a thin pointed piece of metal like a nail with a raised spiral line (called a thread) along it and a line or cross cut into its head. Screws are turned and pressed into wood, metal, etc. with a screwdriver in order to fasten two things together.|to fasten one thing to another or make something tight with a screw or screws|to turn something round and round in order to fasten it in place|to be attached by screwing
gloss_after: noun: metal fastener with spiral thread | verb: fasten with a screw \| twist to tighten

def_before: to close an envelope, etc. by sticking the edges of the opening together|to close a container tightly or fill a small opening, etc., especially so that air, liquid, etc. cannot get in or out|to make something definite, so that it cannot be changed or argued about|an official design or mark, stamped on a document to show that it is real and carries the authority of a particular person or organization|a thing that makes something definite|a substance, piece of material, etc. used to fill a small opening so that air, liquid, etc. cannot get in or out|a piece of wax (= a soft substance produced by bees), soft metal or paper that is placed across the opening of something such as a letter or box and which has to be broken before the letter or box can be opened
gloss_after: verb: close firmly \| make airtight | noun: official stamp \| marine mammal

def_before: to take somebody/something in your hand suddenly and using force|to take control of a place or situation, often suddenly and violently|to arrest or capture somebody|to take illegal or stolen goods away from somebody|to be quick to make use of a chance, an opportunity, etc.
gloss_after: grab suddenly \| take control by force \| seize an opportunity: use it quickly

def_before: a feeling that you get when something affects your body|the ability to feel through your sense of touch|a general feeling or impression that is difficult to explain; an experience or a memory|very great surprise, excitement, or interest among a lot of people; the person or the thing that causes this surprise
gloss_after: physical feeling \| something causing great excitement or interest

def_before: to break something, or to be broken, violently and noisily into many pieces|to move with a lot of force against something solid; to make something do this|to hit something very hard and break it, in order to get through it|to crash a vehicle
gloss_after: break violently into pieces \| hit with great force

def_before: the length of time that something lasts or is able to continue|to last all through a period of time or to cover the whole of it|to include a large area or a lot of things|to stretch right across something, from one side to the other
gloss_after: noun: length of time or distance covered | verb: extend across time or space

def_before: to make something such as time or money available to somebody or for something, especially when it requires an effort for you to do this|to save somebody/yourself from having to go through an unpleasant experience|to allow somebody/something to escape harm, damage or death, especially when others do not escape it|to do everything possible to achieve something or to do something well without trying to limit the time or money involved
gloss_after: make available \| refrain from harming

def_before: two lenses in a frame that rests on the nose and ears. People wear spectacles in order to be able to see better or to protect their eyes from bright light.|a performance or an event that is very impressive and exciting to look at|a sight or view that is very impressive to look at|an unusual or surprising sight or situation that attracts a lot of attention
gloss_after: spectacles: glasses \| an impressive public display

def_before: a short period of time during which something lasts|a period of time doing something or working somewhere|words that are thought to have magic power or to make a piece of magic work; a piece of magic that happens when somebody says these magic words|a quality that a person or thing has that makes them so attractive or interesting that they have a strong influence on you
gloss_after: a short period of time \| magic formula

def_before: a very fast turning movement|the way you make a ball turn very fast when you throw it or hit it|to turn round and round quickly; to make something do this|to turn round quickly once; to make somebody do this
gloss_after: noun: fast turning motion \| biased presentation of information | verb: rotate fast \| put spin on

def_before: pressure on a system or relationship because great demands are being placed on it|mental pressure or worry felt by somebody because they have too much to do or manage; something that causes this pressure|the pressure that is put on something when a physical force stretches, pushes, or pulls it|an injury to a part of your body, such as a muscle, that is caused by using it too much or by twisting it
gloss_after: pressure or stress \| muscle injury from overuse

def_before: to become more powerful or effective; to make somebody/something more powerful or effective|to become physically stronger|to make a feeling, an opinion or a relationship stronger; to become stronger|to become stronger|to become stronger; to make a currency or an economy stronger
gloss_after: make or become stronger or more powerful

def_before: the uniform that is worn by the members of a sports team when they are playing|a street that has many shops, stores, restaurants, etc. along it|to take off all or most of your clothes or another person’s clothes|to remove a layer from something, especially so that it is completely exposed|to remove all the things from a place and leave it empty
gloss_after: noun: long narrow piece \| sports team uniform | verb: remove covering

def_before: to put an end, often by force, to a group or an activity that is believed to threaten authority|to prevent something from being published or made known|to prevent yourself from having or expressing a feeling or an emotion|to prevent something from growing, developing or continuing
gloss_after: forcibly end or prevent from being expressed

def_before: a sudden increase of a strong feeling|a sudden increase in the amount or number of something|a sudden, strong movement forward or upwards|a sudden increase in the flow of electrical power through a system|to move quickly and with force in a particular direction|to fill somebody with a strong feeling|to suddenly increase in value|to increase suddenly
gloss_after: noun: sudden powerful increase | verb: increase or move suddenly with force

def_before: a swinging movement or rhythm|a change from one opinion or situation to another; the amount by which something changes|a seat for swinging on, hung from above on ropes or chains|to move backwards or forwards or from side to side while hanging from a fixed point; to make something do this|to move from one place to another by holding something that is fixed and pulling yourself along, up, etc.|to move or make something move with a wide curved movement|to change or make somebody/something change from one opinion, mood, etc. to another
gloss_after: swinging motion \| shift from one position to another \| to move back and forth

def_before: a device for controlling the flow of water from a pipe into a bath or sink|a light hit with your hand or fingers|to hit somebody/something quickly and lightly|if you tap your fingers, feet, etc. or they tap, you hit them gently against a table, the floor, etc., for example to the rhythm of music
gloss_after: noun: faucet \| light touch | verb: touch lightly \| access a source

def_before: a situation in which people do not trust each other, or feel unfriendly towards each other, and that may cause them to attack each other|a situation in which the fact that there are different needs or interests causes difficulties|a feeling of worry and stress that makes it impossible to relax|the feeling of fear and excitement that is created by a writer or a film director
gloss_after: strained relationship or anxiety \| tightness of a rope or material

def_before: the act of causing somebody severe pain in order to punish them or make them say or do something|mental or physical pain; something that causes this|to hurt somebody physically or mentally in order to punish them or make them tell you something|to make somebody feel extremely unhappy or anxious
gloss_after: noun: deliberate infliction of severe pain | verb: inflict severe pain \| cause great distress

def_before: a long line or series of marks that is left by somebody/something|a track, sign or smell that is left behind and that can be followed, especially in hunting|a path through the countryside|a route that is followed for a particular purpose|to pull something behind somebody/something, usually along the ground; to be pulled along in this way|to walk slowly because you are tired or bored, especially behind somebody else; to go from one place to another without enthusiasm|to be losing a game or other contest
gloss_after: noun: marks or signs left behind \| path through nature | verb: follow behind \| be losing

def_before: a truck, or a container with wheels, that is pulled by another vehicle|a vehicle without an engine, that can be pulled by a car or truck or used as a home or an office when it is parked|a small building for people to live in that is made in a factory and moved to a permanent place|a series of short scenes from a film or television programme, shown in advance to advertise it
gloss_after: container pulled by a vehicle \| short film advertisement

def_before: a piece of equipment for catching animals|a clever plan designed to trick somebody, either by capturing them or by making them do or say something that they did not mean to do or say|to put somebody in a dangerous place that they cannot get out of|to keep somebody in a bad situation that they want to get out of but cannot|to have part of your body, your clothing, etc. held in a place so tightly that you cannot remove it and it may be injured or damaged|to force somebody/something into a place or situation that they cannot escape from, especially in order to catch them
gloss_after: noun: device for catching \| deceptive scheme | verb: catch \| leave with no escape

def_before: the action of turning something with your hand, or of turning a part of your body|an unexpected change or development in a story or situation|a sharp bend in a road or river|to bend or turn something into a particular shape|to bend or turn something into a shape or position that is not normal or natural; to be bent or turned in this way|to turn part of your body around while the rest stays still|to turn your body with quick, sharp movements and change direction often|to turn something around in a circle with your hand|to bend and change direction often|to injure part of your body, especially your ankle, wrist or knee, bending it in an uncomfortable way|to wind something around or through an object|to move or grow by winding around something
gloss_after: noun: turning motion \| unexpected development | verb: turn or wind \| distort

def_before: the act of making a machine, computer system, etc. more powerful and efficient; the more powerful and efficient machine, computer system, etc.|to make a machine, computer system, etc. more powerful and efficient; to start using a new and better version of a machine, system, etc.|to improve the condition of a building, etc. in order to provide a better service|to give somebody a better seat on a plane, room in a hotel, etc. than the one that they have paid for
gloss_after: improvement in quality or version \| to update to a better version

def_before: to make somebody/something less strong or powerful; to become less strong or powerful|to make something less physically strong; to become less physically strong|to become or make somebody become less determined or certain about something|to become less strong
gloss_after: make or become less strong or powerful

def_before: to hit a person or an animal hard with a whip, as a punishment or to make them go faster or work harder|to move, or make something move, quickly and suddenly or violently in a particular direction|to remove or pull something quickly and suddenly|to mix cream, etc. very quickly until it becomes stiff
gloss_after: strike with a whip \| move suddenly

def_before: the act of taking an amount of money out of your bank account|the act of moving or taking something away or back|the act of no longer taking part in something or being a member of an organization|the period of time when somebody is getting used to not taking a drug that they have become addicted to, and the unpleasant effects of doing this
gloss_after: taking money from a bank \| removing troops \| stopping addictive substance

def_before: having the qualities that deserve somebody/something|having qualities that deserve your respect or attention|having good qualities but not very interesting or exciting|typical of what a particular person or thing might do, give, etc.
gloss_after: deserving recognition or a particular thing

def_before: strange and frightening; different from what you are used to|from another country or society; foreign|not usual or acceptable|connected with creatures from another world
gloss_after: strange and unfamiliar \| a foreign national \| from another planet
